# SLO Definitions — 0000003-hook-based-enforcement

*Generated by spec-agent.*

---

## Applicability

This feature delivers framework scripts and SKILL.md files. There is no deployed service, no user-facing endpoint, and no availability SLO in the traditional sense.

The relevant quality indicators are defined at the script level:

---

## Script-Level SLIs / SLOs

| SLI | SLO | Measurement | Notes |
|-----|-----|-------------|-------|
| Hook script execution time (telemetry) | ≤ 3 seconds p100 | AbortController timeout enforced in script | Hard limit; scripts abort and exit 0 at 3s |
| Hook script failure rate (session blocking) | 0% — hooks must never block a session | Exit code observation | Scripts always exit 0; any non-zero exit is a bug |
| gate-write false-positive block rate | 0% — in-scope writes must never be blocked | Manual acceptance testing per REQ-007 | Path matching must be correct for all confirmed component paths |
| gate-write false-negative pass rate | 0% — out-of-scope writes must never pass | Manual acceptance testing per REQ-006 | Exit code 2 must fire for all out-of-scope paths |
| Duplicate phase_start emissions per session per phase | 0 | Telemetry query on session_id + phase | Flag-file guard (REQ-003) enforces this |

---

## Error Budget

Not applicable — no deployed service with an error budget. Hook scripts are local-execution artefacts. Quality is verified through acceptance criteria at each phase of delivery.

---

## Telemetry Data Quality (informational)

The structured-telemetry-mcp server has its own SLOs. The following is informational for this feature's contribution:

- Expected: one `phase_start` event per session per phase
- Expected: one `phase_end` event per session per phase (may lag by one turn)
- Missing events: acceptable in environments where `PLANIFEST_TELEMETRY_URL` is not configured (intentional opt-out)
